title: Fold-Down Serving Station
summary: A wall-hung cabinet with a hinged project-panel front that folds down into a serving shelf, held level by a hook, eye, and jack chain when open.
description:
This is a small wall-hung box with a divider and a shelf inside — build the divider-and-shelf piece first, since it's much easier to fit that small shelf into place before the front panel is on and a nailer can no longer reach inside comfortably. Nail and glue every joint of the basic box, checking for square as you go, then attach the shelving assembly into the box as one piece.

The front is a project panel cut down to fit the opening, hung on two utility hinges along the bottom edge so it swings down and out like a drop-leaf table rather than a door. **A hook-and-eye kit with jack chain is what holds the panel level once it's open** — attach the chain so the panel stops perfectly horizontal, not drooping past level, or anything set on it will slide off.

Mount to the wall with corner braces and screws driven into studs — this cabinet holds real weight once it's open and loaded, so a stud (or a rated anchor if no stud lands right) is not optional.

cutList.note: Cut down to fit the front opening.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two box top/bottom at 18-3/4", two box sides at 22-1/2", one divider at 17-1/4" and one shelf at 10-7/8" from the 1x6. Cut one fold-down front panel at 22-1/2" from the 3/4" project panel, 2 ft x 2 ft. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to one 10 ft 1x6. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Nail and glue the top, bottom, and sides together, adjusting for square as you go.

steps.title: Build and add the divider and shelf
steps.body: Build the divider-and-shelf assembly separately, then attach it inside the box as one piece — this is easier than fitting the shelf in place once the front is on. Finish with paint or stain at this stage if desired.

steps.title: Hang the front panel
steps.body: Attach the project panel front along the bottom edge with two utility hinges so it swings down like a drop-leaf.

steps.title: Add the support chain
steps.body: Install the hook-and-eye kit with jack chain so the panel stops perfectly level when folded down.

steps.title: Mount to the wall
steps.body: Mount with corner braces and deck screws driven into a wall stud, or rated anchors if no stud lands in position.

unresolvedImages.alt: Fold-down serving station mounted on a wall
